original_hypothesis.title: Retroactive Reward Rate Modification via setRewards for Un-Updated Markets
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `setRewards()` function can set `cantoPerBlock[epoch]` for ANY epoch, including past epochs. Since `update_market()` uses lazy evaluation (only processes blocks when called), governance can retroactively change reward rates for past epochs that haven't been consumed yet by any market's update. This allows governance to arbitrarily increase or decrease rewards for specific markets after the fact.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: There is no check in setRewards that `epoch >= block.number`. Governance can call `setRewards(past_epoch, past_epoch, newRate)` to change the rate for a past epoch. If no market has been updated through that epoch yet, the new rate applies retroactively when update_market eventually processes those blocks.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setRewards only validates epoch alignment (`_fromEpoch % BLOCK_EPOCH == 0`), no check that epochs are in the future. Any epoch's rate can be overwritten.

preconditions: Governance calls setRewards for a past epoch
At least one whitelisted market has lastRewardBlock before the modified epoch
The market has not been updated through the modified epoch yet

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No temporal validation on epoch parameters
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: cantoPerBlock[pastEpoch] can be overwritten to any value. Markets with stale lastRewardBlock will use the new rate when eventually updated.
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No check `_fromEpoch >= currentEpoch`, no immutability for past epochs

analysis_questions: Can governance strategically modify past rates to favor specific markets?
How many markets typically have stale lastRewardBlock values?
Does the stated invariant 'total rewards per block should never exceed configured' hold with retroactive changes?
validation_requirements: Test setRewards for past epoch followed by update_market
Verify reward amounts change based on retroactive rate modification
Assess whether this violates the protocol's stated invariant

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Governance sets cantoPerBlock[epoch_100000] = 1e18 initially. At block 300000, governance modifies cantoPerBlock[epoch_100000] = 100e18. Market X, which hasn't been updated since block 50000, eventually gets updated. It receives 100x the originally intended rewards for epoch 100000, potentially draining the contract.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Arbitrary inflation or deflation of rewards for specific markets
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users in markets affected by retroactive rate changes
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Cannot undo once update_market processes the modified rates

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for retroactive rate modification impact

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable by monitoring setRewards calls for past epochs

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
